zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret Goodwin於1570年5月3日在英國英格蘭諾福爾可藍沃斯受洗時，她的父親Francis Goodwin26歲，母親Joan25歲。她於1598年7月16日與Robert Page II在英國諾福爾可布林頓結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和7個女兒的父母。她埋葬在英國諾福爾可奧斯比 聖 瑪格麗特。

nameMeanings.name: Goodwin
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Godwin (Old English Gōdwine). Compare Godwin .
Irish: adopted in Mayo for Ó Goidín, a variant of Ó Godáin, ‘descendant of Godán’, a diminutive personal name from got ‘stammering, lisping’.

History: This name was brought independently to New England by many bearers from the 17th century onward. William Goodwin was one of the founders of Hartford, CT, in 1635 (coming from Cambridge, MA with Thomas Hooker).

variants.name: Goodwyn
meanings: English (East Anglia and Middlesex): variant of   Goodwin  .

variants.name: Godwin
meanings: from the common Old English personal name Godwine, a compound of god ‘god’ + wine ‘friend, protector, lord’, which survived the Conquest. Compare Goodwin , with which it has most probably been confused.
alternatively, the name may occasionally derive from an Old English female personal name Godwynn.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret Goodwin was christened on 3 May 1570, in Ranworth, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom, her father, Francis Goodwin, was 26 and her mother, Joan, was 25. She married Robert Page II on 16 July 1598, in Hemblington, Norfolk, England. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 7 daughters. She was buried in Ormesby St Margaret, Norfolk, England.

ko.St. Michael, Ormesby, Norfolk, England: 영국 잉글랜드 노르폴크 오메스비 스트 믹하얼
ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Goodwin(이)가 1570년 5월 3일 영국 잉글랜드 노르폴크 랜워스에서 침례를 받았을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Francis Goodwin(은)는 26세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Joan(은)는 25세였다. 그녀는 1598년 7월 16일 영국 노르폴크 헤블링턴에서 Robert Page II(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 일곱 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 영국 노르폴크 오메스비 스트 마거릿에 묻혔다.

ru.St. Michael, Ormesby, Norfolk, England: ормесби ст мачаел, норфолк, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret Goodwin была крещена 3 мая 1570 г. в местности ранворт, норфолк, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Francis Goodwin было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Joan, 25. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Robert Page II 16 июля 1598 г. в местности хемблингтон, норфолк, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 7 дочерей. Она была похоронена в местности ормесби ст маргарет, норфолк, Англия.
